title: knex with connection.ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the database TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: knex validates the database server certificate (connection.ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: knex is initialized with connection: { ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } }, disabling validation of the database server's TLS certificate for the whole pool. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database — exposing every query, credential, and row. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the provider's CA via connection.ssl.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No knex() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: knex() does not set connection.ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: connection: { ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } } is pasted into knex config to connect to a managed Postgres/MySQL without wiring the CA. It ships and disables MITM protection on the database.
